在宅勤務や外出先からの業務でファイル共有を安全に進めるために、送信前・共有中・完: A practical checklist for safe file-sharing while working from home or on the go, covering what to check before sending, during sharing, and after completion.
送るファイルは必要最小限か: Is the file being sent kept to the absolute minimum necessary?
ファイルを送った後は、相手が受け取れたか、作業に必要な状態で開けたかを確認します: After sending a file, confirm whether the recipient received it and could open it in the state required for work. In remote work, since you cannot see the other person's screen or connection status, it is hard to notice even if the download fails. For critical documents, confirm with a simple 'I received it' and, if necessary, check the hash value, file name, or page count to ensure there is no mix-up.
リモートワークでのファイル共有は、オフィス内の受け渡しよりも確認すべき点が増えま: File-sharing in remote work involves more things to check than handovers in the office. The recipient is not on the same network, devices and communication environments vary from person to person, notifications are easily buried in chats or emails, and it is hard to see where the file remains after sharing. When these circumstances overlap, even sending a single document can easily lead to minor incidents like misdirection, expiration, referencing old versions, or leaving shared links abandoned.
最初に確認したいのは、送ろうとしているファイルの中身です。リモートワークでは「ひ: The first thing to check is the contents of the file you are trying to send. In remote work, it is easy to make decisions like 'send the whole folder for now' or 'send related materials together,' but the wider the sharing scope, the greater the impact in the event of an information leak. Before sending, select only the files the recipient needs for work, and check that they do not contain unnecessary history, personal information, internal notes, drafts, or past quotation conditions.
また、共有が終わったファイルは残さないことが基本です。自動削除が使える場合は、共: Additionally, the basic rule is not to leave files after sharing is finished. If automatic deletion is available, set an expiration date before sharing. If it is a manual deletion operation, decide whether the sender is in charge of deletion or if the receiver reports after saving. Deciding on the termination conditions for sharing reduces the risk of links being left abandoned.
毎回長い手順書を読んでから共有するのは現実的ではありません。チームで使うなら、次: It is not realistic to read a long instruction manual every time before sharing. When using it as a team, it is convenient to place a short checklist like the following as a template in the chat. If the sender checks it and shares it with the receiver as needed, variation in judgment can be reduced even during remote work.
特に表計算ファイルやプレゼン資料は、非表示シート、コメント、スピーカーノート、変: Particularly in spreadsheets and presentation materials, information may remain in hidden sheets, comments, speaker notes, and revision histories. Determine whether the document should be converted to PDF before sending, or if it needs to be handed over as editable source data. If the recipient only needs to view it, it is safer not to hand over editable files.
重要なのは、共有リンクだけを投げるのではなく、相手が何をすればよいかを一文添える: The important thing is not just to throw the sharing link, but to add a sentence about what the recipient should do. For example, if you convey the purpose and handling together, such as 'This is the PDF for today's confirmation. It only needs to be referenced during the meeting, so no need to save it after confirmation' or 'This is the latest version of the estimate. Please download this to avoid confusion with old files,' the recipient's judgment will align.
リモートワークでは、共有リンクがチャット、メール、議事録、タスク管理ツールなど複: In remote work, sharing links tend to remain in multiple places such as chats, emails, minutes, and task management tools. While convenient, leaving links after they are no longer needed poses a risk. In file-sharing, the basic rule is to make the exposure scope as narrow as possible and keep the available time to the absolute minimum.
共有時：相手が迷わない受け取り方を用意する: During sharing: Prepare a way to receive files that prevents the recipient from getting lost
ファイル名だけで内容と版が分かる状態になっているか: Is the state such that the content and version can be understood from the file name alone?
受け取り確認と共有終了のタイミングを決めているか: Are the receipt confirmation and sharing end timing decided?
保存期間は作業に必要な長さだけにしているか: Is the retention period set to only the length required for the work?
ファイル名に日付、用途、版が入っているか: Does the file name include the date, application, and version?
リモートワークでは「送れること」より「安全に終われること」が重要: In remote work, 'finishing safely' is more important than 'being able to send'
リモートワークのファイル共有は、特別な知識よりも小さな確認の積み重ねが効きます。: For file-sharing in remote work, a stack of small checks is more effective than special knowledge. If you use a temporary sharing tool like FS!QR Code and make checking before sending, during sharing, and after completion a habit, you can increase safety without slowing down speed.
機密性が高い資料は、パスワード保護やID確認を組み合わせると安心です。共有URL: For highly confidential documents, combining password protection and ID confirmation provides peace of mind. Devices like sending the sharing URL via chat and conveying the password via another communication method—not gathering information into a single route—are also effective. In addition, documents for which handovers are completed in a short time do not need a long retention period set. Choose a sharing method on the premise of not leaving files after receipt is finished.
共有相手が作業に必要なファイルだけを選んでいるか: Is the sender selecting only the files the recipient needs for work?
一時共有の考え方を使うと、旧版リンクを長く残さずに済みます。短い期間だけファイル: Using the concept of temporary sharing helps avoid leaving old version links for a long time. If you operate by keeping files for a short period and terminating once the necessary people receive them, you can reduce the possibility of old links being referenced later. The important thing is not to use file-sharing as a storage location, but to treat it as a passage for handovers.
日常運用に落とし込むためのチェックリスト: Checklist for dropping into daily operations
完了後：共有した事実と削除を確認する: After completion: Confirm the fact of sharing and deletion
FS!QRのようにブラウザとQRコードで一時的に共有できる仕組みは、相手にアプリ: A system like FS!QR Code that allows temporary sharing via browser and QR Code Code is a method compatible with remote environments because it saves having to ask the other party to install an app. However, no matter what tool you use, if the operation before and after sharing is ambiguous, safety will decrease. In this article, we organize checkpoints for carrying out file-sharing quickly and reliably during work from home or on the go.
機密情報が含まれる場合、パスワードや別経路連絡を使っているか: Does it use password or separate route communication if confidential information is included?
リモート環境では、共有リンクを送っただけでは作業が止まることがあります。受け手が: In remote environments, work may stop if you just send a sharing link. The optimal way to receive changes depending on whether the receiver checks on a smartphone, downloads on a PC, is on a company-provided device, or a personal device. With FS!QR Code, you can send via URL or show the QR Code Code across the screen, making it easy to guide them to 'please look at this document now' during online meetings.
保護設定：公開範囲と利用時間を短くする: Protection settings: Shorten public range and usage time
非表示データ、コメント、不要な個人情報が残っていないか: Are there any hidden data, comments, or unnecessary personal information left?
リモートチームでは、同じ資料の修正版が何度も共有されることがあります。ファイル名: Remote teams may share modified versions of the same material multiple times. If file names include "Final," "Latest version," "Revised 2," etc., it will be difficult to know which one to look at, and you will end up making decisions based on the old contents. Be sure to include the date and purpose in the file name before sharing, and clearly state "This is the latest version" in chat.
送信前：本当に共有すべきファイルだけに絞る: Before sending: Focus on only the files you really need to share
共有中：最新版と旧版を混在させない: Sharing: Do not mix the latest version and old version
編集用ファイルではなくPDFなどの閲覧用形式で足りないか: Is it sufficient to use a viewing format such as PDF instead of an editing file?
